title: thalictrum flavum subsp. rufinerve
intro: Thalictrum flavum subsp. rufinerve, also known as the meadow rue, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's medicinal properties also include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a valuable remedy for promoting overall health and well-being. Meadow rue can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
